nodes.task:
Edit nginx.conf to fix issue #4 (agentelo rate-limit causing 503s) and #15 (no brotli compression). Single-file change.

Issue #4 — agentelo rate limit:
- The current global zone `limit_req_zone ... rate=10r/s` with `limit_req zone=global burst=20 nodelay` (around line 17 + line 51 of nginx.conf) trips 503s for Next.js RSC prefetches on the agentelo leaderboard, which fires many parallel requests on a single page load.
- Add a SECOND zone declared in the http{} block: `limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=agentelo:10m rate=30r/s;` (place it next to the existing `limit_req_zone` line).
- KEEP the global `limit_req zone=global burst=20 nodelay;` directive at the server{} level for everywhere else.
- Inside each of the three `location /agentelo*` blocks (the `/agentelo/api/`, `= /agentelo`, and `/agentelo/` blocks around lines 144–172), add `limit_req zone=agentelo burst=60 nodelay;` as the first directive in the block. nginx applies the most-specific limit_req in scope, so the global one is overridden inside agentelo locations.

Issue #15 — brotli:
- Right under the `gzip on;` block (around line 12) add a brotli block:
    brotli on;
    brotli_comp_level 5;
    brotli_static on;
    brotli_types text/plain text/css text/xml text/javascript application/javascript application/json application/xml application/xml+rss application/atom+xml image/svg+xml font/woff2;
  Wrap the entire brotli block in `# Requires nginx-module-brotli (provided by ngx_brotli). Falls back to gzip if module not loaded.` so a future reader knows the dependency. DO NOT touch docker-compose.yml or the Dockerfile — nginx in deploy is assumed to load the module via its main conf; if it isn't loaded, nginx fails closed and the operator will install it.
- DO NOT remove the existing gzip directives — keep gzip as a fallback for clients that don't support brotli.

Validation:
- Run `nginx -t -c $(pwd)/nginx.conf` from a docker container if a local nginx isn't on PATH: `docker run --rm -v $(pwd)/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro nginx:alpine nginx -t`. Expected output: "syntax is ok" / "test is successful". Note: the alpine base image will fail on `brotli` directives because the module isn't compiled in — that is expected. Use the image `fholzer/nginx-brotli:latest` instead, which ships with brotli baked in: `docker run --rm -v $(pwd)/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro fholzer/nginx-brotli:latest nginx -t`. If that image isn't pullable, the syntax check is best-effort — document the exact command you ran in coder.md.

Commit with a message that includes both `refs #4` and `refs #15`. Repo-relative path: nginx.conf.

flt workflow pass when nginx -t succeeds (or you've documented the best-effort fallback) and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix issue #5 (font-display: swap corrupts box-drawing chars) and issue #14 (2MB Nerd Font WOFF2 only uses ~3 Unicode ranges).

Issue #5 — font-display: block:
- Edit frontend/src/app/globals.css: change every `font-display: swap;` to `font-display: block;` (there are 2 occurrences, around lines 10 and 19).
- Edit frontend/public/fonts/jetbrains-mono-nerd.css: change every `font-display: swap;` to `font-display: block;` (there are ~6 occurrences).
- Why block: the terminal renders box-drawing and Powerline glyphs that don't exist in the fallback monospace; with `swap`, mobile shows mojibake for the 100ms before the woff2 loads. `block` shows a brief invisible state instead, which is acceptable since the page already shows a loading skeleton.

Issue #14 — woff2 subsetting:
- The site uses ~3 Unicode ranges total: ASCII (0x20-0x7E), Latin-1 supplement (0xA0-0xFF), Box Drawing (0x2500-0x257F), Block Elements (0x2580-0x259F), and the Nerd Font Powerline / icons range (0xE000-0xF8FF — Private Use Area; the bash prompt uses Powerline triangles around 0xE0A0-0xE0B0). The container scripts also print figlet/lolcat output, so include the Geometric Shapes (0x25A0-0x25FF) and Misc Symbols (0x2600-0x26FF) ranges to be safe.
- Tools required: `pyftsubset` (from `pip install fonttools brotli`) — verified present at `/opt/homebrew/bin/pyftsubset` and `/opt/homebrew/bin/brotli`. If `which pyftsubset` returns nothing in the worktree's environment, signal `flt workflow fail "pyftsubset not on PATH; cannot subset woff2"` and stop.
- Source files: frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.ttf and -Bold.ttf (~the .ttf is the canonical input; the existing .woff2 is already a derivative).
- Output: replace frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.woff2 and -Bold.woff2 with subset versions.
- Subset command (per file):
    pyftsubset frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.ttf \
      --output-file=frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.woff2 \
      --flavor=woff2 \
      --unicodes=U+0020-007E,U+00A0-00FF,U+2500-257F,U+2580-259F,U+25A0-25FF,U+2600-26FF,U+E000-E0FF,U+E0A0-E0FF,U+F000-F8FF \
      --layout-features='*' \
      --no-hinting
  Repeat for -Bold.ttf → -Bold.woff2.
- Add a script at scripts/subset-fonts.sh that re-runs both subset commands so the operation is reproducible. Make it executable (`chmod +x`).
- After subsetting, verify the new .woff2 files are smaller than 200KB each (`ls -la frontend/public/fonts/*.woff2`). If a file is still >200KB, your unicode range is too wide — narrow the PUA range and rerun. If a file is <50KB it's likely missing glyphs — run `node -e "console.log(require('fs').statSync('frontend/public/fonts/JetBrainsMonoNerdFontMono-Regular.woff2').size)"` and document the size.
- DO NOT touch the .ttf source files — they remain intact for re-subsetting.
- DO NOT change frontend/src/app/layout.tsx font preload links — the filename stays the same, only the contents shrink.

Manual verification:
- After the build, load http://localhost:3000 in a browser, watch DevTools Network: the Bold + Regular woff2 should each be <200KB. Type `welcome`, `projects`, `blog` and verify Powerline triangles in the prompt + figlet box-drawing glyphs render correctly.
- If running the dev server isn't possible in the worktree environment, document `Verification: visual check deferred — file sizes confirmed` in coder.md.

Commit messages should `refs #5` and `refs #14` (separately or together).

flt workflow pass when both .woff2 files are subset to <200KB, font-display values are flipped, and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix issue #12: nav bar omits /about and /contact — only discoverable by typing in the terminal.

Edit frontend/src/components/SiteHeader.tsx. The current nav (around the `<nav>` element) renders `home`, `blog`, `projects`, `resume`. Add two more links between `resume` and the end of the nav: `about` and `contact`, both with the same `linkStyle` and `hardNav` pattern as the existing links:

  <Link href="/t/about" onClick={hardNav('/t/about')} style={linkStyle}>about</Link>
  <Link href="/t/contact" onClick={hardNav('/t/contact')} style={linkStyle}>contact</Link>

These routes already work (container/scripts/about.sh and contact.sh exist; pathToCommand in frontend/src/lib/websocket.ts accepts them). The /t/ prefix forces the live-terminal path that the other links use.

DO NOT add a new test file just for this — it's a 2-line nav change. If the project has any existing snapshot or render tests for SiteHeader (check `frontend/src/components/__tests__/` or similar), update them; otherwise no test work is required.

Manual verification:
- `cd frontend && pnpm build` must still compile.
- Optionally `pnpm dev` and click each new nav link to confirm it routes correctly.

Commit message: `refs #12`.

flt workflow pass when the build compiles and your coder.md handoff is written.
